questionHtml: Calculate the energy released when one mole of strontium-90 decays to 2% of its original activity forming the stable daughter product.
markschemeHtml:
Idea that the Yttrium half life is much less than Strontium so can assume all Yttrium energy is included. ✓

0.98×6.02×1023×0.52+2.3×106 seen ✓

=1.67×1030 eV

Answer ×1.6×10-19=270 GJ ✓

meta.title: Calculate the energy released when one mole of strontium-90 decays to 2% of its original activity forming the stable daughter product.
